t_ch: 哈哈，同学再见！记得订阅我的节目，让你有个美好的睡眠……
t_en: Haha, see you later, young one! Don't forget to subscribe to my show for a good night's sleep...

t_ch: 又是头脑空空的一天…构思出真正的艺术还是太难了点。啊，对了——
t_en: Another day with an empty mind... Coming up with real art is still a bit too hard. Ah, right...

t_ch: 同学，你有什么好点子吗？拜托了，我的毕业设计就靠你了！
t_en: Hey, got any bright ideas? Please, I'm counting on you for my graduation project!

t_ch: 突然就是这么重力的话题？
t_en: Why such a heavy topic all of a sudden?

t_ch: 哎呀，这不是没有什么想法嘛——放心，我现在逢人就问，什么样离谱的建议都收到过！我给你举几个例子啊……
t_en: Oh, it's not like I don't have any ideas. Don't worry, I've been asking literally everyone I meet, and I've gotten all sorts of wild suggestions! Let me give you a few examples...

t_ch: 唐尼先生建议过头顶甜甜圈的天环族，安吉拉小姐建议过半夜的电视机，安诺茵小姐建议过大脑切片，泰科老师建议过自然常数的级数展开式——是不是乍一听都还蛮酷的？
t_en: Mr. Donny suggested Halovians with donuts floating above their heads, Miss Angela suggested TVs at midnight, Miss Anoine suggested brain slices, and Mr. Tyker suggested the series expansion of Euler's number... Sounds pretty cool when you first hear it all, right?

t_ch: 但我转念一想，这些东西没一个能表现出我渴望的那种深度！当画布从一片空白中沾染上颜色，如果它们没能获得比空白更加深邃的艺术内涵，这种创作就太失败了！
t_en: But then I thought about it, none of these things could express the depth I yearn for! When colors stain a blank canvas, if they fail to achieve an artistic meaning more profound than the original emptiness, then such creation is an utter failure!

t_ch: …才不是因为我自己眼高手低什么都不敢尝试呢，哼。
t_en: ...It's not like I'm too proud to try anything new or anything, hmph.

t_ch: 画星穹列车，大家都喜欢。
t_en: Everyone loves the Astral Express. Just draw it.

t_ch: 大家都喜欢的东西可画不得！不仅主题容易重复，你个性化的表达也很容易被掩盖掉。我的确也喜欢星穹列车啦……
t_en: You can't just draw what everyone likes! Not only would the themes get repetitive, but your personal artistic expression would also get buried. I mean, sure, I like the Astral Express too...

t_ch: 可是《苍天航路绒绒号》已经这么优秀，不管我怎么画，好像都会被归类为同人作品…这可是毕业设计唉，要是给老师们留下一种「缺乏原创性」的印象，我可就拿不到像样的推荐信了！
t_en: But Fluffy Across the Blue is already so amazing, no matter how I draw it, it'll probably be labeled as fan art... This is my graduation project we're talking about! If I give the professors the impression that I lack originality, I can kiss a decent recommendation letter goodbye!

t_ch: 来个正二十四胞体镶嵌画？
t_en: How about a <unbreak>24</unbreak>-cell polytope tessellation?

t_ch: …呃，你说什么？正二十四胞体？四维空间的那个？唔，总觉得泰科老师也给过这个建议——因为我完全搞不懂，他就改为展示自然常数的级数展开式了！
t_en: ...Uh, what did you say? A <unbreak>24</unbreak>-cell polytope? The one from four-dimensional space? Hmm, I feel like Mr. Tyker suggested that too, but since I couldn't understand it at all, he switched to demonstrating the series expansion of Euler's number instead!

t_ch: 四维空间的艺术实在是太过超前啦。在这个学校里也只有泰科老师才能理解吧。
t_en: Four-dimensional art is way too ahead of its time. I guess Mr. Tyker is the only one at this school who can understand it.

t_ch: 交张白纸上去应该不错。
t_en: Turning in a blank paper should do the trick.

t_ch: 喔，你很勇哦！理论上说，这的确是行为艺术的一种，大概真的也可以当作毕业设计吧……
t_en: Oh, you're brave! Theoretically speaking, this could indeed be considered performance art... It might actually work as a graduation project...

t_ch: …才怪咧。老师们又不是傻子。只有白纸才会承认白纸天然是白纸的作品，这一点白纸还是非常明白的！
t_en: ...As if! The professors aren't stupid. Only I, whose name means white paper, would admit that a blank paper is the true art form of a white paper. That's something that I am absolutely certain of!

t_ch: 啊，一口气说了好多「白纸」，对不起。
t_en: Ah, sorry for rambling on about "paper" so much.

t_ch: 没想法，改天再聊。
t_en: No ideas right now. Let's chat some other time.

t_ch: 改天聊哦！
t_en: Let's chat another time!

t_ch: 没问题，我来出主意！
t_en: No problem, I've got ideas!

t_ch: 室中幽暗，昏昧灯光无法给人带来任何暖意，反倒格外唤起心中的不安感。
t_en: The room is dim, its murky light bringing no warmth, instead stirring up an unsettling feeling deep within.

t_ch: …是…谁…咯咯…咯……
t_en: ...Wh—Who... h—he... he...

t_ch: 恶灵退散——！！！
t_en: Begone, evil spirits!!!

t_ch: 诶呦——吓到你了真是对不住！别打了！手下留情啊同学！！！
t_en: Oops, sorry for scaring you! Stop hitting me! Have mercy, friend!!!

t_ch: …好家伙，现在搞恶作剧的风险这么高了吗？差点小命搭在这…幸好我本来就是幽灵，不用再死一次了，哈哈……
t_en: ...Holy moly, has pulling pranks become this risky now? I almost lost my life there... Good thing I'm already a ghost, so I don't have to die twice, haha...

t_ch: 嗨，这位同学，看你刚刚的反应，我这恶作剧应该还挺成功的，对吧？
t_en: Hey there, friend! Judging by your reaction, I'd say my little prank was quite successful, wouldn't you agree?

t_ch: 作业不饱和是吧？
t_en: Not getting enough homework for you, huh?

t_ch: 坏了，碰上行家了——你这话可比我的恶作剧恐怖多了！
t_en: Oh snap, I've met my match. What you just said is way scarier than my pranks!

t_ch: 看来是我下手轻了…
t_en: Looks like I went too easy on her...

t_ch: 别别别，就你这力道，要是再来两下，我可真就变尘灵了！
t_en: Whoa whoa whoa, with that much force, a couple more hits, and I'll literally turn into a wispae!

t_ch: 事情还要从几天前的一个晚上说起…为了缓解考试前巨大的压力，我原本打算在宿舍里来个恐怖电影之夜，结果看着看着，灵感不期而至——
t_en: It all started a few nights ago... To relieve the immense pressure before exams, I planned to have a horror movie night in my dorm. And as I was watching, inspiration struck out of nowhere...

t_ch: 受到那位从镜子里爬出来的美女姐姐启发，我决定用同样的方式潜入教员室，借阅一下这次的考题……
t_en: Inspired by that pretty lady who crawled out of the mirror, I decided to sneak into the faculty room the same way and... uh, "borrow" a peek at the exam's questions...

t_ch: 谁问你这个了…
t_en: I wasn't even asking about this...

t_ch: 你没问我就不能讲了吗？现在又不是课堂，还得举手发言…总之先听我说完！
t_en: Do I need your permission to talk about it? This isn't a classroom where we have to raise our hands... Just let me finish what I'm saying!

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 然后…然后我刚探出头来，就和值班老师撞了个正着…想笑就笑吧，我不介意，毕竟我也想笑。
t_en: And then... and then just as I poked my head out, I ran into the teacher on duty... Go ahead and laugh all you want. I don't mind. I mean, I kinda feel like laughing too.

t_ch: 虽然被全校通报还扣了学分，但彼时彼刻，那位老师的惊叫声为我打开了一扇通往新世界的大门——
t_en: Even though I got called out in front of the whole school and lost credit points, at that very moment, that teacher's scream opened up the door to a new world for me...

t_ch: 自此，我找到了人生…哦，是幽灵生的真正意义——给大家枯燥无味的生活带来一点刺激。
t_en: And so, I found the meaning of life... oh, I mean ghost-life, that is, to spice up people's boring existence a little bit.

t_ch: 总之，说了这么多，我要表达的意思只有一个——虽然目前经验尚浅，但等到下次见面，我一定会给你个惊喜！
t_en: Long story short, after all that talk, I only have one thing to say: Even though I'm still a bit green, I'll definitely give you a surprise you won't forget next time we meet!

t_ch: 什么玩意？！溜了溜了——
t_en: What the heck!? I'm outta here...

t_ch: 哎哎哎——等会儿？！怎么就跑了啊……
t_en: Hey, wait up! Where are you running off to...

t_ch: 嗨——同学，又见面啦！惊不惊喜，意不意外？
t_en: Hey there, friend! Long time no see! What a surprise, bet you didn't expect this!

t_ch: 你不怕鞋柜里有脚气吗？
t_en: Aren't you afraid of the athlete's foot odor in your shoe cabinet?

t_ch: 呃啊…不要说这么可怕的话啊！
t_en: Ugh... Don't say such scary things!

t_ch: （伸手去关柜门）
t_en: (reaches to close the cabinet door)

t_ch: 诶？诶！等等——
t_en: Huh? Hey! Wait...

t_ch: 可恶，你这家伙，怎么比我还不按常理出牌啊……
t_en: Darn it, you... you're even more unpredictable than I am!

t_ch: 个人推荐芥末口味的汽水，一口下去提神醒脑，保证你涕泗横流~
t_en: I highly recommend the wasabi-flavored soda, a sip of which will wake you up like nothing else, guaranteed to make your eyes water and nose run~

t_ch: 你们幻造种口味这么独特吗？
t_en: Do all of you imagenae have such... unique tastes?

t_ch: 倒也不是…主要是姐们儿生前就严格自律，绝不喝纯净水以外的饮料，结果压抑了一辈子口腹之欲，最后还是英年早逝、香消玉殒。
t_en: Not exactly... It's mostly that back when I was alive, I was a stickler for self-discipline. I wouldn't touch a drop of anything except purified water. I spent a whole lifetime suppressing my cravings, only to die young and beautiful anyway.

t_ch: 现在姐们儿彻底想通了！人生短暂，不尝遍酸甜苦辣咸，不是枉费眼耳鼻舌身吗！来来来，你也来喝一口这芥末汽水吧！
t_en: But now, I've seen the light! Life is short. If you don't experience every flavor life has to offer: sour, sweet, bitter, spicy, and salty, then aren't you just wasting your senses? Come on, come on! You've gotta try a sip of this wasabi-flavored soda!

t_ch: （踢一脚贩卖机）
t_en: (kick the vending machine)

t_ch: 哎哎哎，轻点！要散架了——
t_en: Hey, hey, hey, take it easy! I'm falling apart here...

t_ch: 咦，你怎么在这？我还没藏好呢…虽然不喜欢搞得浑身湿漉漉的，但「潜伏在水下伏击路过的行人」——这主意不错吧？
t_en: Huh, what are you doing here? I haven't found a good hiding spot yet... Though I'm not a fan of getting all soaked, lurking underwater to ambush passersby isn't a bad idea, no?

t_ch: 需要我帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need any help?

t_ch: 嗯？帮忙？帮什么……
t_en: Huh? Help? With what...?

t_ch: （将人按进水里）
t_en: (dunk passersby underwater)

t_ch: 说好的动口不动手呢——有人谋杀幽…咕噜噜噜噜噜——
t_en: What happened to "use words, not fists"? Someone's trying to kill ghos— *gurgle* *gurgle* *gurgle*...

t_ch: 咳咳…咳——好样的，这次是我技不如人，你给我等着！
t_en: *cough* *cough*... Well played, you got me this time, but just you wait!

t_ch: 哟，这不是咱亲爱的同学吗？上课辛苦，快来逛逛小卖部，今天想买点什么？
t_en: Well well, look who it is! Long day of classes? Take a break and check out the General Store. What can I get for you today?

t_ch: 买点学生该买的。
t_en: Just the usual school stuff.

t_ch: 想要来点特别的。
t_en: Show me something... interesting.

t_ch: 真有眼光，我AAA送货私我开的「AAA」小卖部，贩卖各类好物，正经、不正经的都管够！
t_en: Excellent choice! Here at AAA Delivery, Contact Me, we've got all kinds of goodies. Whether you're looking for something by the book or... off the books, we've got you covered!

t_ch: 你怎么叫这么个名字？
t_en: How'd you end up with a name like that?

t_ch: 你懂什么，这可是时兴的潮流！
t_en: What do you know? This is the latest trend!

t_ch: 自打我发现，打上AAA，就可以让自己的名字永远屹立于所有人通讯录之巅、达到万众瞩目之效后，我一拍大腿，义无反顾地选择给自己直接改了名。
t_en: I realized that slapping AAA in front of my name would keep me permanently perched at the top of everyone's contact list: achieving maximum center of attention status. The moment I realized that, I smacked my thigh in revelation and changed my name on the spot without a second thought.

t_ch: 现在，我不仅是通讯录最上头的男人，还是位列「绘世学院」校友录、「企业家年会」花名册、「小摊小贩俱乐部」名录…等等等等，所有榜单统统第一的男人。
t_en: Now, I'm not only the guy at the top of the contacts list, but also the alumnus of Graphia Academy, on the roster of "Entrepreneurs Annual," a member of the "Hawker's League" and so on... If there's a list, I'm at its summit.

t_ch: 毫不夸张地讲，就是上寰宇通缉榜，只要它按首字母排序，我也能勇夺第一~
t_en: No joke, even if the galaxy's most wanted list were sorted alphabetically, I'd still snag the top spot!

t_ch: 至于我的小卖部…前阵子那个「琥珀王A级评选」你关注了没有？哼，不怕告诉你，人们拼命厮杀拿到的「琥珀王三A推荐」，也只是见我的门槛罢了！
t_en: As for my General Store... Did you catch that "Amber Lord Grade-A Selection" a while back? Heh, I'll let you in on a secret: People fight tooth and nail for that "Triple-A Amber Lord Recommendation," and that's just the bare minimum to get through my door!

t_ch: 不正经…有多不正经？
t_en: Off the books... how far off the books are we talking about?

t_ch: 不怕告诉你，在我这儿，可以买到你学生时代最美好的幻梦、做人这辈子最大的勇敢、对父母这一生最好的交代——
t_en: I'll let you in on a secret. Right here, you can buy the wildest fantasy of your school days, the biggest dose of courage you'll ever need, and the one thing that'll make your parents proud for the rest of your life!

t_ch: 上道点儿，过来，耳朵凑过来…
t_en: Come on, get over here. Closer...

t_ch: 只要<unbreak>188</unbreak>，「AAA考试*辅助器*」带回家！
t_en: For just 188, you can take home the "AAA Exam-Assister"!

t_ch: 这你不被抓？
t_en: You're not gonna get caught selling that?

t_ch: 因为我同步也给老师卖「AAA干扰器」，那个只要<unbreak>88</unbreak>！
t_en: Because I also sell "AAA Jammers" to the teachers! And those are only <unbreak>88</unbreak>!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 没问题，欢迎下次惠顾，记得好好学习、天天向上！
t_en: You got it. Come back anytime! And don't forget to study hard and keep your grades up!

t_ch: 唉，看着这群学生，我是真嫉妒啊。大家都能在画布上挥洒自己的灵感，不像我，早已发誓封笔……
t_en: *sigh* Looking at these students, I'm so envious. They can all pour their inspiration onto the canvas, while I... I've sworn to never pick up a brush again...

t_ch: 绘艺学校的老师竟然不画画？
t_en: An art school teacher who doesn't even paint?

t_ch: 我又不教绘艺，我是历史老师…艺术史也是很重要的学问哦，「幻造」不是无根之木，更不是凭空捏造，必须理解过往的文脉才行。
t_en: I don't teach art, I'm a history teacher... Art history is also a crucial field of study, you know. Imagenesis has its roots, and cannot possibly be conjured from pure fiction. You need to understand the cultural context of the past.

t_ch: 我以前也是画画的，但后来发生了一些事，让我不得不封笔。你感兴趣吗？这也算是一段关于我个人的「艺术史」，哈哈哈！
t_en: I used to be an artist too, but then something happened that forced me to put down my brush. Wanna hear about it? It's kind of my own little "art history," haha!

t_ch: 快讲来听听。
t_en: Spill the beans!

t_ch: 你知道火花小姐吧？二相乐园最伟大的行为艺术家。当我遇到她时，就意识到，我自年少开始接受的一切绘艺训练，都是为了如今能尽可能还原出火花小姐的魅力！
t_en: You know Miss Sparxie, right? The greatest performance artist in Planarcadia. When I met her, I realized that all my artistic training since childhood was meant for this moment: to capture Miss Sparxie's charm as perfectly as possible!

t_ch: 火花小姐的魅力并不在于容貌，而在于那独一无二的、开天辟地的、弃绝重力束缚的可爱灵魂。所以为了用画笔还原出这份复杂的魅力，我创造了一种全新的表现手法。
t_en: Miss Sparxie's charm isn't about her looks, but about her unique, groundbreaking, gravity-defying adorable soul. That's why, to capture this complex allure with my brush, I've created a completely new artistic approach.

t_ch: 这种表现手法，我称之为「心相写真」。我融合了一些两面三刀主义的解构技法，和纯粹感觉至高无上主义的几何图形，再加上一些精神分析学层面上对火花小姐的潜意识刻画……
t_en: I call this expressive technique "Psycho-Portraiture." I've blended some deconstructionist double-dealing techniques with pure sensory supremacist geometry, plus some psychoanalytical depictions of Miss Sparxie's subconscious...

t_ch: 最终我绘制出了一系列巨作——《某神性少女的肖像画》，整整二十九副！虽然只有极少部分人能看出我画的是火花…哎，不被人欣赏，本来就是艺术的宿命。
t_en: Finally, I created a series of masterpieces, Portraits of a Divine Girl, twenty-nine pieces in total! Though only a handful of people could tell I was drawing Sparxie... Well, being unappreciated is the fate of an artist.

t_ch: 好在，这个系列引起了火花小姐本人的注意。一个冷寂的夜晚，我正在绘制《某神性少女的肖像画》的第三十副作品时，我的电脑屏幕突然亮了起来。
t_en: Fortunately, this series caught Miss Sparxie's attention. On a cold, quiet night, while I was working on the thirtieth piece of Portraits of a Divine Girl, my computer screen suddenly lit up.

t_ch: 紧接着，火花小姐竟然从我的电脑屏幕中爬了出来！还没等我缓过神来，她就走进了我的房间，轻柔地扼住了我的咽喉，在我耳边温润地说道——
t_en: Then, out of nowhere, Miss Sparxie crawled right out of my computer screen! Before I could even process what was happening, she walked into my room, gently wrapped her hands around my throat, and whispered in my ear...

t_ch: 「宝宝，你再画你那个破画，我就要找人弄死你了哦～」
t_en: "Darling, if you keep drawing that crappy art of yours, I'm gonna have someone take you out~"

t_ch: 就这样，我发誓封笔，不再画画，成为了一名艺术史老师。
t_en: And that's how I swore off drawing forever and became an art history teacher instead.

t_ch: 乔尔乔老师的声音开始哽咽。他抬起头，努力不让眼泪流下来。看起来他已经彻底沉浸在回忆之中了。现在还是不要打扰他了。
t_en: Mr. Joerjo's voice begins to choke up. He lifts his chin up, trying hard not to let the tears fall. He seems to be completely lost in his memories now. Better not disturb him.

t_ch: 上课还是免了吧。
t_en: I'll pass on going to class.

t_ch: 无妨，这也确实不是一段值得到处讲给别人听的历史。
t_en: Don't worry about it. It's not exactly the kind of history worth sharing with everyone anyway.

t_ch: （不和他搭话）
t_en: (Ignore him)

t_ch: 这位同学，不要只顾着修炼绘艺，文化课也是很重要的哦。
t_en: Hey there, don't just focus on honing your art skills. Your cultural studies are important too, you know!

t_ch: 可恶啊…又一次失败了！现在的年轻人，篮球水平越来越高，连我的必杀角色「暴龙勿用」都对付不了他们了……
t_en: Argh... Another loss! These kids today, their basketball skills just keep getting better and better. Even my ultimate character "Tyrant Dragon Dormant" can't handle them anymore...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 不，没什么，只是迎来了又一次失败而言。对于我「败者之魂」大鹰而言，这早已是常态。
t_en: Nah, it's nothing, just another failure to add to the list. For me, Eagle, the Soul of the Loser, this is already business as usual.

t_ch: 但每次遭遇新的失败，我的内心都仍然会产生强烈的不甘…也正是这种不甘，促使我投身下一场战斗，并遭逢下一次失败。
t_en: Yet each time I fall, that bitter taste of defeat still burns deep within... And it's precisely that same bitterness that drives me toward the next battle, only to meet the next failure.

t_ch: 话虽如此，但我已经成为了上届「幻月游戏」的败者之一。这可不是一般人能获得的殊荣。这样算来，我又何尝不是一种「败者冠军」呢？
t_en: That said, I am already one of the losers in last year's Phantasmoon Games. That's not a title just anyone can claim. So in a way, doesn't that make me a "Champion of Defeat"?

t_ch: 又见面了。你此番前来有什么事吗？难道说…是来击败我的？
t_en: We meet again. What brings you here this time? Could it be... you're here to defeat me?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 聊，都可以聊。成功的经验我没有，失败的经验我愿倾囊相授。
t_en: A chat? I can do that. I don't have any success stories, but I'm more than happy to share all my experiences with failure.

t_ch: 什么是「败者之魂」？
t_en: What is the Soul of the Loser?

t_ch: 这个篮球场，乃是胜败交织之地。若干年来这里展开过无数对决，有些只是训练，有些则纠缠着双方的宿命。
t_en: This basketball court is a place where victory and defeat intertwine. Over the years, countless showdowns have unfolded here: some are mere practice, others are battles of destiny.

t_ch: 而凡是对决，必有胜负。有胜者的地方，就必有败者。
t_en: And wherever there is a duel, there must be a winner. Where there is a winner, there must also be a loser.

t_ch: 每一位败者，都心怀不甘。这些不甘的「愿」，单独拿出来也许微不足道，但经年累月、水滴石穿，就聚集成了我——「败者之魂」大鹰。
t_en: Every loser carries an unwavering desire. These unfulfilled "wishes" might seem insignificant on their own, but over the years, like water wearing away stone, they've all coalesced into me: Eagle, the Soul of the Loser.

t_ch: 我并非幽灵，但也并非寻常的幻造种。你将我理解成「愿力的钟乳石」就好。
t_en: I'm not a ghost, but neither am I an ordinary imagenae. Just think of me as a pillar of Wishpower.

t_ch: 你参加过幻月游戏？
t_en: You've participated in the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 没错！我曾参加过上一届幻月游戏，这大概是我漫长生涯中最值得称道的成就！
t_en: That's right! I participated in the last Phantasmoon Games. It was probably the most noteworthy achievement in my entire career!

t_ch: 话虽如此，但其实我才刚参加游戏，就被咱们学校那个红发小姑娘给打败了……
t_en: That being said, I had just joined the game when I got beaten by that red-haired girl from our school...

t_ch: 我甚至都没怎么来得及向公众展示自己高超的篮球三绝——亢龙不悔、战龙在野和暴龙勿用，就这么做个无人在意的炮灰。现在想想，也还是心有不甘。
t_en: I never even got the chance to show off my three legendary basketball moves to the public — the Soaring Dragon's Might, Dragon's Dominance, and Dragon's Fury — before being reduced to nothing. Looking back, I still feel a pang of resentment.

t_ch: 但有两件事，让我一直对命运心怀感激。其一，是我退场足够早，躲过了告死魔事件，捡回了一条小命。
t_en: But there are two things that make me forever grateful to destiny. First, I got out early enough to dodge the Herald of Death incident, saving my own skin.

t_ch: 其二，是我选择参与幻月游戏这件事本身。我总是心怀不甘，总是渴望胜利，所以才会有机会参与游戏，并从中获得独一无二的生命体验。
t_en: Second, it was my choice to participate in the Phantasmoon Games. My constant dissatisfaction and thirst for victory gave me the chance to take part in the games, through which I had a truly unique life experience.

t_ch: 你大可嘲笑我是精神胜利法，但在我看来，单从这一点上来说，在乐子神的游戏中，我绝不算是一个败者。
t_en: Go ahead and mock me for rationalizing my way to victory, but the way I see it, at least on this one point, I'm no loser in The Laughter's game.

t_ch: 胜败毕竟只是瞬息一合，而「生命体验」才是长青金树。
t_en: Victory and defeat are but fleeting moments. It's the experience of life itself that endures like an everlasting golden tree.

t_ch: 你的失败听起来简直像因果律。
t_en: Your failure sounds like pure causality.

t_ch: 也许吧，也许真的是某种「因果律」。也许这是乐子神的玩笑，也许这种某种命运的机制，也许败者怨念所凝结成的大鹰，就是注定无法获得胜利。
t_en: Maybe it really is some kind of causality. It could also be a joke from The Laughter, some mechanism of destiny, or perhaps it's just the eagle, formed from the resentment of the defeated, is simply fated never to achieve victory.

t_ch: 但我选择不相信这样的「注定」。
t_en: But I choose not to believe in such a destiny.

t_ch: 武者的生存意义不在于胜利本身，而在于对胜利的「饥渴」。我会贪婪地渴求胜利，直到自己得到它。
t_en: A samurai's meaning of existence lies not in victory itself, but in the hunger for victory. I will greedily crave victory until I claim it for myself.

t_ch: 其实医务室的安诺茵老师也告诫过，我维系存在的核心就在于「败者的不甘」，一旦我获得胜利，恐怕就会烟消云散吧？
t_en: Actually, Miss Anoine from the infirmary warned me about this too. My very existence is sustained by the loser's regret. If I ever achieve victory, I guess I'll probably just... vanish into thin air?

t_ch: 但即使胜利就意味着死亡，我也仍然渴望胜利。
t_en: Then again, even if victory means death, I still yearn for it.

t_ch: 哈哈，这恐怕就是我这种运动白痴的愚蠢之处吧。
t_en: Haha, I guess that's just the foolishness of a clumsy jock like me.

t_ch: 不聊了。
t_en: That's it for today.

t_ch: 再见！祝愿你能获得胜利。
t_en: See you! Hope you emerge victorious!

t_ch: 不聊了。
t_en: That's it for today.

t_ch: 再见！祝愿你能获得胜利。
t_en: See you! Hope you emerge victorious!

t_ch: …契…约…魔法……
t_en: ...C—Contract... Magic...

t_ch: 对对，这次好多了！再来一遍，就按照我刚刚教你的说！
t_en: Yep, that's much better! Let's do it one more time, just like I taught you!

t_ch: …魔法…少女……契…约…约…约…约……
t_en: ...Magical... Girl... C—C—C—Contract...

t_ch: （听听他们在聊什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 怎么还卡带了…唉，尘灵怎么这么难训练？我到底为什么会想要训练尘灵作为自己的搭档…真是超级大笨蛋。
t_en: Why did it freeze up again... *sigh* Why are wispae so hard to train? What was past-me thinking, wanting to train a wispae as my partner... I'm such an idiot.

t_ch: 你…超级大笨蛋……
t_en: You... You idiot...

t_ch: 嘿！你这小东西！教你魔法少女搭档的台词，怎么也学不会，结果骂人的话倒是一学就会啊！
t_en: Hey! You little rascal! I try to teach you proper magical girl partner lines, you just can't get them right, but you somehow pick up insults just like that!

t_ch: 肌肉…大笨蛋……
t_en: Muscle-headed... Idiot...

t_ch: 怎么还变着花样骂起来了？别瞧不起这身肌肉——你知道我为它们付出了多少辛劳和汗水吗？
t_en: Now you're getting creative with the insults? Don't you dare look down on these muscles! Do you have any idea how much blood, sweat, and tears I put into them?

t_ch: 就为了能获得媲美「魔法」的强大力量，我已经在学校的{RUBY_B#健身社团}「类固醇飞升社」{RUBY_E#}坚持锻炼三个月了！
t_en: Just to gain power that rivals "magic," I've been working out for three months at the {RUBY_B#Bodybuilding Club}Steroid Amplifying Club{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: …五…大三粗…四肢…发达…肌肉…不是魔法！
t_en: ...Burly oaf... Blockheaded jock... Muscles... is not magic!

t_ch: 没完了是吧？果然，跟你这种不长脑子的生物简直没法交流！指望你理解我的梦想，还不如去幻想在幻月游戏面见阿哈呢……
t_en: Are you ever gonna stop? As expected, it's impossible to communicate with a brainless creature like you! Expecting you to understand my dreams is even more far-fetched than fantasizing about meeting Aha at the Phantasmoon Games...

t_ch: …梦？想…少女…魔…法？
t_en: ...Dream? M—Magical girl?

t_ch: 就是我成为魔法少女的梦想啊！还记得《星球☆防卫少女》吗？就是那个魔法少女抵抗外星入侵的游戏，我最喜欢的游戏。
t_en: It's my dream of becoming a magical girl! Remember Planetary☆Defense Girls? That game where magical girls fight against alien invasions? It's my absolute favorite game!

t_ch: 少、女…星…？
t_en: Girl... Planet...?

t_ch: 我到底为什么要和尘灵聊游戏…总之那个游戏里说，魔法少女们的崇高精神必须有人继承——所以你懂了吧？我一定得成为魔法少女才行！
t_en: Why on earth am I talking about games with a wispae... Anyway, that game said the noble spirit of magical girl must be inherited, so you get it now, right? I must become a magical girl!

t_ch: 来，再跟我说一遍——「和我签订契约，成为魔法少女吧」！
t_en: Come on, say it again: "Make a contract with me and become a magical girl!"

t_ch: 什么乱七八糟的？你这家伙脑子有毛病吧？
t_en: What kind of nonsense is this? Are you out of your mind?

t_ch: 怎么骂街的时候突然就说话流畅了！
t_en: How did your speech suddenly become so fluent when you're cursing me out?

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: 你好同学，想了解关于二相乐园的历史吗？嘘…我们小声点，别让唐尼听到。
t_en: Hey there, friend! Want to learn about Planarcadia's history? Shh... let's keep it down, don't let Donny hear us.

t_ch: 为什么不能让他听到？
t_en: Why can't we let him hear us?

t_ch: 是这样…我是来自匹诺康尼的留学生丹娜。为了当上历史课代表，我准备了许多冷门的历史小知识，准备在课上大显身手！
t_en: So here's the deal... I'm Dana, an exchange student from Penacony. To become the class rep for History, I've been prepping tons of obscure historical trivia so I can totally show off in class!

t_ch: 唐尼是我的竞争者，绝对不能让他知道我在研究什么。来，我悄悄给你讲……
t_en: Donny's my competition. I can't let him find out what I'm working on. Come here, let me fill you in on the down-low...

t_ch: 众所周知，在二相乐园，妖怪是通过绘艺创生出来的。
t_en: As everyone knows, in Planarcadia, strangelings are brought into being through art.

t_ch: 但我有个猜想：其实异空间中本来就有无数妖怪，绘师绘画的过程只是一个召唤仪式，只不过召唤出的妖怪和画作长得很像罢了。
t_en: But I have a theory: there are actually countless strangelings that already exist in unusual dimensions, and an artist's painting process is just a summoning ceremony. It just so happens that the summoned strangelings look a lot like the artwork.

t_ch: 好有道理！
t_en: That makes so much sense!

t_ch: 对吧对吧！有位著名的绘师也说过：「妖怪本天成，妙手偶得之」。算上你和他，现在有三个人认同我的观点了！
t_en: Right, right!? A famous artist once said: "Strangelings are born from the sky, discovered by virtuosos." Counting you and him, that's three people who agree with me now!

t_ch: 只能说…很新奇。
t_en: All I can say is... it's quite the novelty.

t_ch: 那就对了！只要能震慑住班上的同学就行。
t_en: Exactly! As long as it blows other students' minds, that's all that matters.

t_ch: 其实我的研究还不止如此——
t_en: Actually, my research goes way beyond that.

t_ch: 听说过假面愚者成为无名客，并让列车坠毁的传闻吧——其实直到车厢因气压升高而爆炸那天，他才明白自己每天操控的是进气阀而不是方向盘。
t_en: You've heard the rumor about a Masked Fool who became one of the Nameless and crashed the Express, right? Truth is, it wasn't until the day the car exploded from excessive air pressure that he realized what he'd been operating every day was an intake valve, not a steering wheel.

t_ch: 还有，假面愚者和悲悼伶人原本不分彼此。但在一桩偷窃案后，被偷了面具的一群人整日以泪洗面，就成了悲悼伶人；而偷到了面具的则沾沾自喜，成了假面愚者。
t_en: Oh, and the Masked Fools and Mourning Actors weren't always separate groups. But after a robbery, those who had their masks stolen spent their days drowning in tears and became the Mourning Actors, while those who stole the masks were smug with satisfaction and became the Masked Fools.

t_ch: 对了对了，整座二维市在天上看其实是一幅巨大的二维码，据说只要用手机扫描就能跳转神秘网址，和阿哈网上热聊！
t_en: Oh oh, and get this, the entire Duomension City looks like one giant QR code from the sky! Rumor has it, if you scan it with your phone, it takes you to some mysterious website where you can chat it up with Aha online!

t_ch: 这些可都是惊天大料啊，你听了算是赚到了。
t_en: This is some seriously juicy intel, consider yourself lucky to be in on it.

t_ch: 不感兴趣
t_en: Not interested.

t_ch: 好吧…但记住，别信唐尼的鬼话，他就是个阴谋论者。
t_en: Alright... but remember, don't believe a word Donny says. He's just a conspiracy theorist.

t_ch: 想听点二相乐园「真正」的历史吗？对了，建议你别理旁边那人。
t_en: Wanna hear about the "real" history of Planarcadia? Oh, and I'd suggest ignoring that person next to you.

t_ch: 你们俩有什么矛盾？
t_en: What's the beef between you two?

t_ch: 这位丹娜想和我争夺历史课代表的位置，但很可惜…最终的赢家一定属于我唐尼。
t_en: That Dana girl wants to compete with me for the position of history class representative. Unfortunately for her, the ultimate victor will definitely be me, Donny.

t_ch: 因为我已经发现了一个惊天大秘密。
t_en: Because I've already uncovered a mind-blowing secret.

t_ch: 你或许已经注意到了…二相乐园的幻造技术和朋克洛德的以太编辑有诸多相似之处。
t_en: You may have already noticed... Planarcadia's imagenesis technology and Punklorde's aether editing have quite a few similarities.

t_ch: 同样是将想象化为现实的技术，它们之间毫无关系的可能性极小，所以…只存在两种可能——
t_en: Both being technologies that turn imagination into reality, the chances of them being completely unrelated are slim to none, so... there are only two possibilities.

t_ch: 幻造技术参考了以太编辑？
t_en: Imagenesis tech drew from aether editing?

t_ch: 原来你也这样想！
t_en: So you think so too!

t_ch: 以太编辑参考了幻造技术？
t_en: Aether editing references imagenesis technology?

t_ch: 恰恰相反，幻造技术不过是对以太编辑的拙劣模仿。
t_en: Quite the opposite: Imagenesis technology is nothing more than a crude imitation of aether editing.

t_ch: 依我看，一切都是公司的阴谋：他们窃取了以太编辑技术，并用于建设二相乐园。
t_en: If you ask me, it's all the IPC's conspiracy: They stole aether editing technology and used it to build Planarcadia.

t_ch: 公司无法插手朋克洛德的运转，于是想要再造一个新的朋克洛德——这就是二相乐园，一个被打上公司烙印的人造游乐场。
t_en: The IPC couldn't get their hands on Punklorde's operations, so they decided to build a whole new Punklorde from scratch: which is Planarcadia, an artificial playground stamped with the IPC's branding.

t_ch: 说到这我就来气——许多骇客被公司开出的价码吸引，开始为他们提供技术支持。
t_en: Speaking of which, that irks me. So many hackers got lured in by the IPC's fat paychecks and started providing them with tech support.

t_ch: 如今谁还会记得：虽然二相乐园产出的游戏如今风靡银河，但朋克洛德才是游戏这一媒介的发源地……
t_en: Who even remembers now: Sure, Planarcadia's games have taken the cosmos by storm, but Punklorde is where the medium of gaming actually originated from...

t_ch: 他们称呼的「绘艺」更是欲盖弥彰：其实质就是换了个名字的「编程」技术！
t_en: And that so-called "art" they keep going on about? Total smoke and mirrors! It's just programming with a fancy new name!

t_ch: 总之，作为朋克洛德人，我的目的就是搜集公司窃取朋克洛德技术的证据。为此，我计划一步步爬上高位。
t_en: Anyway, as a Punklordian, my goal is to gather evidence of the IPC stealing Punklorde technology. To do that, I plan to climb my way up the ranks, step by step.

t_ch: 就从我成为班上的历史课代表开始。
t_en: It all starts when I become the history class rep.

t_ch: 不感兴趣
t_en: Not interested.

t_ch: 给你个建议，别去找丹娜聊天，她讲的全是野史。
t_en: Here's a tip: Don't go chatting with Dana, everything she says is just tall tales and conspiracy theories.

t_ch: 你好，同学，来医务室是哪里不舒服？
t_en: Hi there. What brings you to the infirmary? Are you feeling unwell?

t_ch: 想让医生帮我瞧瞧…
t_en: I was hoping the doctor could take a look at me...

t_ch: 嗯…
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 大体明白了。你体内似乎有异物…能量体，不，更特殊的某种事物？暂时好像不会产生什么严重影响。
t_en: I think I get the gist of it. There seems to be a foreign object in your body... An energy form? No, something more peculiar? It doesn't seem like it will have any severe effects for the time being.

t_ch: 这种情况靠手术没法处理，很遗憾，不然我就现场把你切开治好。
t_en: A condition like this can't be treated with surgery. It's a pity, otherwise I'd just put you under and fix you up right here and now.

t_ch: 想在这休息可以自便，暂时别来打扰我就行，等下我还有一个开颅手术要做。
t_en: You're welcome to rest here if you need to. Just don't disturb me for the time being. I have a craniotomy scheduled for later.

t_ch: 您真的是校医？
t_en: Are you really the school doctor?

t_ch: 如假包换的校医，毕业于哈托彼亚医科大学，持有幻造医师资格证，顺便一提，我是治愈系的。
t_en: Yes. A bonafide doctor. I graduated from Ahatopia Medical College and am a licensed imagenesis physician. And for the record, I specialize in healing.

t_ch: 选择这里是因为薪水稳定，可以准点下班——当然，要比学生们晚一个系统时就是了。
t_en: I chose this job because the salary is stable and I can clock out on time, though I do have to stay one system hour later than the students, of course.

t_ch: 给学生开颅？
t_en: Craniotomy? On students?

t_ch: 确切地说，三位愿力过敏的不同种族学生，因为看了太多虚拟主播，导致松果体萎缩了。
t_en: On three students from different species suffering from Wishpower allergies, to be exact. Their pineal glands have atrophied from watching too many VStreamers.

t_ch: 在二维市，这是比感冒还要常见的「流行」病。
t_en: It's actually an "epidemic" more common than a cold in Duomension City.

t_ch: 不多聊了，接下来我打算挑战一下将手术时间压缩在午休<unbreak>30</unbreak>分钟内，这样不会耽误学生下午上课。
t_en: Anyway, enough chatting. I'm gonna challenge myself to squeeze the surgery into a <unbreak>30</unbreak>-minute lunch break, so it won't mess with the students' afternoon classes.

t_ch: 没事，我只是路过。
t_en: It's nothing. I was just passing through.

t_ch: 一切正常？那回去吧，这里对你应该没什么价值。
t_en: Everything's alright? Then head on back. There's likely nothing of value for you here.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，好久不见。上次那个案件，实在是给你们添麻烦了。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, it's been a while. I apologize for all the trouble with that last case.

t_ch: 你没被取消行医执照？
t_en: You haven't had your medical license revoked?

t_ch: 还是不能搞灰产啊…
t_en: Still can't get into those shady businesses...

t_ch: 唉…我找一位叫暗布雷拉的律师帮忙斡旋，最后被罚了很多钱，但好歹保住了诊所和饭碗。
t_en: *sigh* ...I got a lawyer named Umbralle to help mediate and ended up paying a ton of money in fines, but at least I managed to keep the clinic and my job.

t_ch: 往后你身边如果有哪位幻造种亲朋需要医疗服务，请直接联系我，我愿意免费治疗。
t_en: If any of your imagenae friends need medical assistance in the future, please contact me directly. I'm willing to treat them, free of charge.

t_ch: 那多谢你的好意了。
t_en: Well, thanks for your kindness.

t_ch: 别这么说，都只是我微不足道的补偿罢了。
t_en: Don't mention it, it's just my humble way of making amends.

t_ch: 别了，我怕再遇到杀手。
t_en: Nope, I'm out. Don't wanna run into any more killers.

t_ch: …从统计学上来说，同一家诊所出现两位连环杀手的概率应该不高。
t_en: ...Statistically speaking, the probability of having two serial killers at the same clinic should be pretty low.

t_ch: 另外，如果你对「幻造生理学」感兴趣，也可以找我聊聊。我应该能提供不少帮助。
t_en: Also, if you're interested in Imagenesis Physiology, feel free to chat with me about it. I should be able to help quite a bit.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，又见面了。请问有什么需要帮忙的吗？
t_en: Nice to see you again, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. How may I help you today?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 你想聊什么话题呢？
t_en: What would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 幻造种也有生老病死吗？
t_en: Do imagenae go through the cycle of birth, aging, sickness, and death too?

t_ch: 那是必然。幻造种虽说诞生自人类的幻想之中，但同样也是生命，凡是生命就脱离不了生老病死。
t_en: Of course. Although imagenae are born from human imagination, they're still living beings, and like all life, they can't escape the cycle of birth, aging, sickness, and death.

t_ch: 不过，幻造种的躯体由「愿力」构成，患有的疾病也自然和有机生命大不相同。
t_en: However, since the bodies of imagenae are made of Wishpower, their illnesses are naturally quite different from those of organic life forms.

t_ch: 有机生命的疾病往往来自生物性因素、代谢问题、免疫问题等等，而幻造种的疾病，则往往来自「愿力」本身的失调。
t_en: While organic life forms' diseases often stem from biological factors, metabolic issues, immune problems, and so on, the diseases of imagenae typically originate from imbalances in their Wishpower itself.

t_ch: 举例来说，有一种疾病叫做「反皮格马利翁症候群」。
t_en: For example, there's a condition called Malignant Pygmalion Syndrome.

t_ch: 简单来说，就是幻造种疯狂的迷恋上了自己的造主，要求对方「无条件的爱上自己，无条件的奉献自己的爱」。在某些极端情况，甚至会导致幻造种杀死自己的造主。
t_en: In simple terms, the imagenae becomes obsessively infatuated with their creator, demanding unconditional love and complete devotion of their affection. In extreme cases, this can even lead to the imagenae killing their own creator.

t_ch: 这种病的本质是被造物对于造物主的类似「原始崇拜」的情感。而如果无法克制和妥善处理这种情感，就会走向极端。
t_en: The essence of this condition is a primitive worship-like emotion that creations feel towards their creator. And if this emotion cannot be counteracted and properly managed, it will lead to extremes.

t_ch: 从这个例子中也能看出来，幻造种常见的疾病，实际上更接近于有机生命的「心理与精神疾病」。
t_en: From this example, you can see that common diseases among the imagenae are actually more similar to psychological and mental disorders found in organic life forms.

t_ch: 但区别是，你不会因为思觉失调而多长出几条不听使唤的肢体，而对于幻造种来说，这甚至是比较常见的症状。
t_en: But the difference is, you won't grow extra uncontrollable limbs due to thought disorder, while for the imagenae, that's actually a pretty common symptom.

t_ch: 要怎么给幻造种治病呢？
t_en: How do you treat a sick imagenae?

t_ch: 唔…如何治病这件事，如果要用专业术语来解释，那三两句话可解释不完。我好歹也是上了快十年医学院，才出来开诊所的。
t_en: Hmm... for that matter, if we're talking technical terms here, it's not something I can sum up in a few sentences. I mean, I spent nearly a decade in medical school before opening my clinic, after all.

t_ch: 「反叙事干预」「逻辑重构」「逆模因转录」「共情矩阵」…光是这些专有名词就够我解释个七天七夜。
t_en: "Anti-narrative Intervention," "Logic Refactoring," "Reverse Memetic Transcription," "Empathy Matrix"... Just explaining these technical terms alone would take me seven days and nights.

t_ch: 如果用不精准但简单的方式来解释——我们这些幻造种医生做的事，本质上和绘师们做的事差别不大。只不过他们是绘画的人，而我们是补画的人。
t_en: To put it in simple, imprecise terms, what we imagenae doctors do isn't all that different from what artists do. The only difference is that they're the ones who paint, while we're the ones who touch up the painting.

t_ch: 复原经年劣化的陈旧画作、补救原作者的作画失误、拼接碎裂的画作、拆分黏连的画布…简单来说，就是这样的工作。
t_en: Restoring aged and deteriorated artworks, fixing the original artist's mistakes, piecing together fragmented paintings, separating stuck canvases... Essentially, that's what the job is about.

t_ch: 比如一个为和平而生的幻造种，却不得不频繁诉诸暴力，那就很可能因为「叙事矛盾」而产生种种疾患。
t_en: For example, if an imagenae born for peace is forced to frequently resort to violence, they may develop various afflictions due to the "narrative contradiction."

t_ch: 这种时候，医生就要探寻这位患者的文脉，重新厘清他的叙事逻辑，并帮助他找到「一致性」和「自洽」。
t_en: In times like these, the doctor needs to explore the patient's narrative pulse, re-examine their storytelling logic, and help them find consistency and coherence.

t_ch: 说到底是一个枯燥的工作，但还挺有成就感的。
t_en: It's a tedious job at the end of the day, but still pretty rewarding.

t_ch: 千禧杀手的案子对你影响大吗？
t_en: Did the Millennium Killer case affect you a lot?

t_ch: 唉，一提起这事我就觉得难受。我那时觉得米兰达医生在背地里做的灰产和我无关，只要她能为诊所提供价值就好，所以一直视而不见。
t_en: *sigh* It still makes me sick just thinking about it. Back then, I thought Dr. Miranda's shady side business had nothing to do with me, so I just turned a blind eye as long as she was bringing value to the clinic.

t_ch: 谁曾想，她是一个那么疯狂且病态的人…如果我早点意识到这一点，也许很多被害人都不会死了。
t_en: Who would've thought that she was such a crazy and twisted person... If I had realized this sooner, perhaps many victims wouldn't have died.

t_ch: 你只能自己承担责任了。
t_en: You'll have to take responsibility for this yourself.

t_ch: 唉，如果我尽己所能，为这座城市做出些贡献，也许能算是一点补救吧。
t_en: *sigh* If I do my best and make some contributions to this city, maybe that could count as a small redemption.

t_ch: 你也很难料到事情会变成这样。
t_en: You couldn't have seen this coming, either.

t_ch: 谢谢你安慰。但正因为没人能料想到事情会如何发展，才应该始终做出无愧良心的决定…这是我学到的重要教训。
t_en: Thanks for the comfort. But precisely because no one can predict how things will turn out, one should always make decisions that leave one with a clear conscience... That is an important lesson I've learned.

t_ch: 这件事之后，我被异常防御部罚了很多钱，诊所也差点关张，但现在好歹是撑了下来。
t_en: After that incident, I was fined a ton of money by the Department of Aberration Defense, and my clinic almost had to shut down, but somehow I managed to keep it running.

t_ch: 我失去了患者的信任，收入锐减，现在其实过得比较艰苦。但这不怪任何人，都是我咎由自取。
t_en: I lost my patients' trust, my income plummeted, and now I'm actually having a pretty rough time. But I don't blame anyone. I brought this upon myself.

t_ch: 而且，即使经营状况再糟糕，我也还是会继续维持诊所运营的。毕竟当缩头乌龟解决不了任何事，只有堂堂正正面对自己行为的后果，才有机会夺回患者的信任。
t_en: Besides, no matter how badly the clinic is doing, I'll keep it running. After all, hiding like a coward won't solve anything. Only by facing the consequences of our actions head-on can we have a chance to win back our patients' trust.

t_ch: 不聊了。
t_en: That's it for today.

t_ch: 再见。记得我的承诺：往后你身边如果有哪位幻造种亲朋需要医疗服务，请直接联系我，我愿意免费治疗。
t_en: Goodbye. Remember my promise: If any of your imagenae friends or family need medical assistance in the future, please contact me directly. I'm willing to treat them, free of charge.

t_ch: 不聊了。
t_en: That's it for today.

t_ch: 再见。记得我的承诺：往后你身边如果有哪位幻造种亲朋需要医疗服务，请直接联系我，我愿意免费治疗。
t_en: Goodbye. Remember my promise: If any of your imagenae friends or family need medical assistance in the future, please contact me directly. I'm willing to treat them, free of charge.

t_ch: 你好，面善的朋友，轮值酒保钟珊竭诚为您服务！
t_en: Hello there, friendly face! I'm Zhongshan, your duty drinksmith, at your service!

t_ch: 本店小本买卖，客人想要饮用特调，请备好信用点，单杯五万，小费十万起……
t_en: We run a modest business here. If you want a special blend, make sure you have enough credits. It's <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> per drink, and tips start at <unbreak>100,000</unbreak>...

t_ch: 世界的尽头原来是通货膨胀。
t_en: So the end of the world is just inflation.

t_ch: 客人，千金难买你开心呐！咱虽然是小本买卖，但快乐无价是不是？
t_en: My dear customer, your happiness is worth more than gold! I may run a humble business, but joy is priceless, am I right?

t_ch: 哈哈，开个玩笑！在「世界尽头」，欢愉无需代价，一个有趣的笑话就能换一杯佳酿！
t_en: Haha, just kidding! At the "World's End" Tavern, the elation comes free. A good joke is all you need to earn yourself a drink!

t_ch: 你人怪好嘞，明明可以直接抢…
t_en: That's very generous of you, since you could've just robbed me...

t_ch: 哦哟，客人别急，这就是个玩笑！
t_en: Hey, come on, I was just joking!

t_ch: 在「世界尽头」酒馆，犯罪可比不过找乐，一个笑话就能换一杯佳酿啦！
t_en: At the "World's End" Tavern, fun trumps crime any day. A good laugh is worth a glass of fine wine!

t_ch: 欸，不过，仔细一瞧，我倒觉得客人你有几分眼熟啊……
t_en: Hey, but now that I take a closer look... you seem kinda familiar...

t_ch: 大善人「放生」你来啦！
t_en: Well well, if it isn't our generous "Unshackled"!

t_ch: 乐子神在上！我这双被酒沫熏晕了的眼，竟然没认出您就是我那「放生」搭档！
t_en: By The Laughter! My eyes must've been too clouded by these beer foam to recognize my dear "Unshackling" partner!

t_ch: 我就说我们会在酒馆重逢！上次那个放生的笑话真令人难忘……
t_en: I knew we'd meet again at the tavern! That joke about unshackling last time was truly unforgettable...

t_ch: 不过这回，等着被放生的人倒成我了，有乐子吧？
t_en: Though this time, it looks like I'm the one waiting to be unshackled. Funny, isn't it?

t_ch: 对，我就是「银河棒球侠」！
t_en: Yeah, I am the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 对对对！就是你！《苍天航路绒绒号》的主角，闻名二相乐园的「球棒浣熊」！
t_en: Right! You! The protagonist of Fluffy Across the Blue, the one and only "Baseball Raccoon" famed throughout Planarcadia!

t_ch: 乐子神在上，你一定有很多有趣的冒险笑话吧！请一定要讲给我听！
t_en: By The Laughter! You must have tons of hilarious adventure jokes, right? Please, do share some with me!

t_ch: 哎，对我这样四处找乐的游子来说，留在酒馆轮值跟坐牢有什么区别？可恨命运偏要戏弄钟珊珊！
t_en: Ah, for a free spirit like me who lives for laughter, being stuck on tavern duty might as well be prison time! Cruel fate, to toy with poor Zhongshanshan!

t_ch: 要想脱手酒保这苦差事，如今我只有三条路可以走——
t_en: To get out of the miseries of being a drinksmith, I've only got three options left...

t_ch: 「一、交<unbreak>23333</unbreak>万赎金；二、写<unbreak>23333</unbreak>个令人捧腹的笑话；三、死。」
t_en: "One, pay <unbreak>23,333</unbreak> million in ransom; two, write <unbreak>23,333</unbreak> knee-slapping jokes; three, die."

t_ch: 选一。
t_en: Pick one.

t_ch: 要不您出钱？
t_en: Maybe you'd like to foot the bill?

t_ch: 选二。
t_en: Pick two.

t_ch: 还得是您呐！
t_en: I knew I could count on you!

t_ch: 选三。
t_en: Pick three.

t_ch: 非得我出命？
t_en: Do I look like I want to die for this?

t_ch: 总之我又穷又嫌乐子没耍够，最后只能编笑话咯！
t_en: Long story short, I'm broke and haven't had my fill of fun, so all I can do is to write jokes!

t_ch: 可自从停止冒险，我这个游子的灵感也宣告枯竭，到今天也才写了<unbreak>23</unbreak>条。
t_en: But ever since I stopped adventuring, my muse also vanished into thin air! I've only managed to write <unbreak>23</unbreak> jokes to this day.

t_ch: 我需要灵感，需要冒险，需要充满激情的乐子！但「世界尽头」里的笑话居然越来越无聊…欸，这句话倒挺好笑……
t_en: I need inspiration! Adventure! That passionate, thrilling fun! But the jokes at "World's End" Tavern are just getting more and more boring... Hey, now that's actually funny...

t_ch: 面善的朋友，你愿不愿意用冒险之旅中的笑话交换一杯佳酿？
t_en: Say, familiar friend, how about trading one joke from your adventure for a fine drink?

t_ch: 先用后付也可以。饮下一杯笑话酿出的特调，你定能哈哈大笑，与我畅聊！
t_en: Pay-later works for me. Take a sip of my joke-brewed special, and I promise you'll laugh out loud, then we can chat the night away!

t_ch: 有什么推荐？
t_en: Any recommendations?

t_ch: 我未成年，就聊聊天吧。
t_en: I'm underage. Let's just chat.

t_ch: 乐子神在上，自打做了酒保，我可难得有机会和一个真正清醒的人说话！想聊点什么？
t_en: By The Laughter, since I became a drinksmith, I rarely get to talk to someone who's actually sober! So, what do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 关于你怎么做了酒保…
t_en: About how you became a drinksmith...

t_ch: 想听命运枷锁套住我钟珊珊的故事？行吧，就当博您一乐。
t_en: Wanna hear the story of how fate shackled me? Alright, let me entertain you with that one.

t_ch: 咱们酒馆一直是轮值制度，上一任酒保布拉琪离开后，酒馆召集寰宇的假面愚者来抽签，谁抽中最长的谁就是倒霉蛋。
t_en: Our tavern has always run on rotation. When the last drinksmith, Bragi, left, the tavern gathered all the Masked Fools across the cosmos to draw lots. Whoever drew the longest stick got stuck behind the counter.

t_ch: 我那时候刚跑出仙舟，满手大包小包，抽了签没地方搁，就胡乱收了起来。
t_en: I had just run out of the Xianzhou with my arms full of bags and packages, so when it came time to draw, I just grabbed one and stuffed it away without looking.

t_ch: 那签也就是根短木棍，食指长不到，绝不会是那倒霉催的最长一根！
t_en: That stick was just a tiny twig, barely the length of my index finger. There's no way in hell it was the longest one!

t_ch: 但是轮着该出示的时候，我居然死活找不见它，翻了半天，您猜我最后从包里掏出根什么？
t_en: But when it was my turn to show it, I just couldn't find it no matter what! After rummaging around forever, guess what I finally pulled out of my bag?

t_ch: ——<size=28>七寸长的仙舟筷子</size>！
t_en: ——<size=28>A seven-inch long Xianzhou chopstick</size>!

t_ch: 你是不是觉得，「按常识来说」这个结果不该算数？我也这么觉得！但在酒馆，「常识」不重要。这个结果足够「好笑」，所以我就被迫上任咯～
t_en: You probably think, "logically speaking," this shouldn't count, right? I feel the same way! But logic doesn't matter here in the tavern. The result was "funny" enough, so, here I am, stuck with the job eternally~

t_ch: 为什么不想做酒保？
t_en: Why don't you like being a drinksmith?

t_ch: 人人都有自个儿的欢愉之道，一句话概括，我是个「游历派」的假面愚者。
t_en: Everyone has their own path to Elation. In a nutshell, I'm an "Excursion-style" Masked Fool.

t_ch: 不怕跟您说，在戴上面具之前，我就是个普通边星上再普通不过的普通人，可别觉得我在饶舌，世上大多数人都这样，虽然不是主角，但也没空找乐！
t_en: Truth be told, before I ever put on the mask, I was just another Average Joe from some backwater planet. And I'm not just running my mouth here, that's how it is for most folks out there. We're not the stars of the show, but hey, who needs fun when you're busy just getting by?

t_ch: 我小时候忙着懂事，长大了忙着读书，再大点开始忙着找工作，不仅乐子少得可怜，稀里糊涂回头一看——嚯，我甚至从没走出自家那颗小边星！
t_en: When I was young, I was too busy maturing. Growing up, I was too busy studying. Then I got even older and got busy job hunting. Not only did I miss out on all the fun, but when I look back now... whoa! I'd never even left my own little planet!

t_ch: 直到公司开拓部大驾光临，通讯器把寰宇的娱乐传遍我们那豆丁点大的星球，我才知道外头的世界那么大，生活琐碎之外还有那么多乐子。
t_en: It wasn't until the IPC Marketing Development Department graced us with their presence that I realized just how vast the outside world was. It wasn't until their communicators brought the galaxy's entertainment to our tiny speck of a planet that I saw how many interesting stufff was beyond life's trivialities.

t_ch: 这也好玩，那也有趣，那为什么不四处看看？于是我钟珊珊，伪装成公司职员溜上船，开始冒险了！
t_en: So much to enjoy, so much to see! What's stopping me? That's how I, Zhongshanshan, snuck aboard disguised as an IPC employee and started my adventure!

t_ch: 你舍弃了从前的一切。
t_en: You gave up everything from your past.

t_ch: 哦哟，听过那句话没？「要不计代价地追求快乐！」
t_en: Oh boy, have you ever heard that saying? "Pursue happiness at any cost!"

t_ch: 但做酒保也有乐子。
t_en: But being a drinksmith has its fun too.

t_ch: 乐子神在上，没人比你更懂欢愉！
t_en: By The Laughter, no one understands joy better than you!

t_ch: 其实我并不讨厌做酒保，我只是讨厌「一直」做酒保。
t_en: I don't actually hate being a drinksmith. I just hate being a drinksmith FOREVER.

t_ch: ——因为我的伪装三天就被识破，给开拓部免费调了几年的特调！
t_en: Because my disguise was seen through in just three days, I ended up making special drinks for the Marketing Development Department for free for years!

t_ch: 「世界尽头」酒馆现在的笑话…
t_en: Jokes at the "World's End" Tavern...

t_ch: 诶哟，悄悄告诉您，那真是不如当年！您瞧，现在这儿除了烂醉的愚者，就是假装清醒的公司成员。
t_en: Psst, let me tell you a secret: It's no longer what it used to be! Look around, all you see nowadays are either drunk Fools or IPC members pretending to be sober.

t_ch: 假面愚者为什么烂醉？因为无聊，太无聊！过去借助公司的技术，咱也算过了阵好日子，人在酒馆坐，全寰宇的乐子却都能瞧见，每天笑到快掉眼泪。
t_en: Why is the Masked Fool drunk? Because of boredom, sheer boredom! Back when we had IPC's tech, life was pretty sweet. Just sit in the tavern, and we could witness all the fun stuff across the universe, rolling on the floor laughing every single day.

t_ch: 但日子一长，再好笑的东西也变得无聊，真正让人开怀的乐子更是难找，所以他们开始狂欢，喝老笑话制的佳酿，指望从中找到更刺激的笑话。
t_en: As time dragged on, even the funniest things got boring, and genuine laughs became harder to find. So they started getting merry, drinking wine brewed from stale jokes, hoping to discover more exciting punch lines in their drunken haze.

t_ch: ——结果乱七八糟的东西倒从胃里呕吐了出来！听听，这是他们昨天抱着马桶边吐边讲的新笑话：
t_en: And then all that mess came spewing out of their stomachs! Get this, here's the new joke they were telling while hugging the toilet and throwing up yesterday:

t_ch: 「我今早猛踹了瘸子右边的好腿！」「为什么？」「——因为昨天才踹过他左腿！」
t_en: "I kicked a limping guy's uninjured right leg this morning!" "Why would you do that?" "Because I just kicked his left leg yesterday!"

t_ch: 他们吐完，整个酒馆哄堂大笑，但笑完以后呢？
t_en: After they finished puking, the whole tavern burst into laughter, but what happened after the laughter died down?

t_ch: 冲了。
t_en: They flushed everything down the toilet.

t_ch: 哈哈，没错，「一次性笑话」就还给马桶吧！
t_en: Haha, right. That one-liner got flushed down the toilet where it belongs!

t_ch: 要我说，咱还是得跑出门，到人堆里去，做点事整个活冒些险，最后齐乐乐众乐乐，那才是真乐子！
t_en: If you have to ask, I say we gotta get out there, dive into the crowd, do some stuff, stir up some action, take some risks, and have a blast together. Now that's what I call the real fun!

t_ch: 至于那帮公司员工，哦哟更可怜，听笑话都只听纯现实主义。你只有大喊一声：「大厂、五险一金、周末双休不加班！」
t_en: As for those IPC workers, oh boy, they're even more pitiful. They only appreciate pure realism jokes. All you gotta do is shout: "Big tech company! Full benefits! Weekends off and no overtime!"

t_ch: ——他们才能发自内心哈哈大笑，放心醉倒见阿哈。
t_en: Only then can they laugh heartily from the bottom of their hearts and feel assured to get drunk and meet Aha.

t_ch: 听说在这能见阿哈？
t_en: I heard people can find Aha here?

t_ch: 酒馆里戴着面具的愚者这么多，谁能说里面没有乐子神？
t_en: With so many Masked Fools in the tavern, who's to say The Laughter isn't among them?

t_ch: 再说，见不见阿哈，都不耽误咱找乐子。毕竟不论饮用佳酿还是参与冒险，咱都是奔着大笑一场，再「阿哈」一声去的。
t_en: Besides, whether we meet Aha or not won't stop us from having fun. After all, whether we're sipping fine wine or going on adventures, we're here for some laughs and sweet moments.

t_ch: 如果你非要亲眼见他，喏，出门左拐，报名参加「幻月游戏」去——不对，你好像已经报名了，哈哈。
t_en: If you really wanna see him in person, well, take a left at the door and sign up for the Phantasmoon Games. Oh wait, you've already signed up, haha.

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 没问题，朋友，下次记得带上你的冒险笑话来换特调！但愿我能早点编完那些段子……
t_en: No problem, friend! Next time, bring your adventure jokes to trade for a special drink! Hope I can finish writing those punch lines soon...

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 没问题，朋友，下次记得带上你的冒险笑话来换特调！但愿我能早点编完那些段子……
t_en: No problem, friend! Next time, bring your adventure jokes to trade for a special drink! Hope I can finish writing those punch lines soon...

t_ch: 艾珂姐，下个月，我就要结婚了…真的很感激你，帮我度过了我生命中最艰难的时期。
t_en: Miss Aco, I'm getting married next month... I'm truly grateful to you for helping me through the toughest time of my life.

t_ch: 哈哈，恭喜你啦！姐姐打心底里为你感到开心~
t_en: Haha, congratulations! I'm truly happy for you from the bottom of my heart~

t_ch: 但你知道，我的恋人是位欧克，等到结婚之后，可能就没机会了。所以，这就是最后的时机！我一定要对你说出口！
t_en: But you know, my lover is an orch, and after we get married, I might not get another chance. So this is my last opportunity! I absolutely must tell you how I feel!

t_ch: …我在听。
t_en: ...I'm listening.

t_ch: 艾珂姐，我！我会把我单身的朋友都介绍来！请务必给我们算团购价！
t_en: Miss Aco, I—I'll introduce all my single friends to you! Please give us a group discount!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，又撮合成一对，后续的生意也不愁啦。看着有情人终成眷属的感觉可真好啊…你说是吧，朋友~
t_en: Hehe, another match made! Future business is looking bright too. It feels so good to see lovebirds end up together... Don't you think so, my friend?

t_ch: 我还以为有狗血剧能看呢。
t_en: And here I thought I'd get to watch some juicy drama.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，刚刚我自己也给吓到了。但结果好就好！
t_en: To be honest, I scared myself just now too. But hey, all's well ends well!

t_ch: 所以你是做婚介生意的？
t_en: So you're in the matchmaking business?

t_ch: 没错！幻造种婚恋咨询中心「幻缘小屋」，店长艾珂，有缘还请多多照顾生意！
t_en: That's right! I'm Aco, owner of Dreamlink House: the Imagenae Dating Consultation Center. Looking forward to earning your patronage!

t_ch: 不过朋友，你看起来像个万人迷，不像是会使用我们家业务的人…要不然，我帮你测试一下，你最适合跟哈托彼亚哪个种族的幻造交往？怎么样？
t_en: But friend, you look like quite the charmer, not really the type who'd need our services... Hey, how about I help you find out which race of imagenae from the Ahatopia would be your perfect match? What do you say?

t_ch: 详细讲讲？
t_en: Care to elaborate?

t_ch: 我们店主打的是「跨种族」承接结缘业务，但要知道这里的幻造种族，真的是五花八门……
t_en: Our shop specializes in cross-species matchmaking services, but you should know that the imagenae races here are truly a mixed bag of everything under the sun...

t_ch: 为避免乱点鸳鸯谱，我们会为第一次来的客人提供免费测试，帮助我们了解对方的喜好，精准匹配到适合的有缘人。
t_en: To avoid any matchmaking mishaps, we offer free compatibility tests for first-time visitors, helping us understand their preferences and precisely match them with their perfect cosmic connection.

t_ch: 怎样，有兴趣吗？
t_en: So, interested?

t_ch: 后面有机会吧。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，感情之事不可强求，我会在这呆一阵子，有兴趣就来找我吧！
t_en: Mhm, you can't force matters of the heart. I'll be hanging around here for a while, so feel free to drop by if you're interested!

t_ch: 欢迎光临「幻缘小屋」分店！
t_en: Welcome to this branch of the Dreamlink House!

t_ch: 不对…在酒馆里谈生意…总感觉哪不对劲…嗯？
t_en: Something's not right... Making deals in a tavern... Something just feels off about this... Hm?

t_ch: 我想做一次恋爱测试！
t_en: I want to take a love test!

t_ch: 没问题！但…测试开始前请允许我重审：*本测试仅供娱乐，不代表任何官方观点或建议*。
t_en: No problem! But before we begin the test, please let me clarify: * This test is for entertainment purposes only and does not represent any official views or recommendations*.

t_ch: 三问中的第一问：如果您因不可抗力即将约会迟到，您会选择以下哪种解决方案？
t_en: First of the three questions: If you're running late for a date due to circumstances beyond your control, which of the following solutions would you choose?

t_ch: 把全城的交通信号灯黑掉。
t_en: Knock out all the traffic lights in the city.

t_ch: 把整个餐馆买下来当做赔礼。
t_en: Buy out the entire restaurant as an apology.

t_ch: 这都什么选项啊！
t_en: What kind of options are these!?

t_ch: 不要怀疑，这测试可是很权威的！由心理大师夏谢德•明梓亲自认证过呢！
t_en: Don't doubt it, this test is super legit! It's even certified by the psychological guru Xasiad Minza herself!

t_ch: 好的——第二问：下面有两件物品，只能选一样随身携带，您会选择——
t_en: Alright, second question: There are two items below, and you can only carry one with you. Which would you choose?

t_ch: 削铁如泥的指甲刀。
t_en: A nail clipper that cuts through steel like butter.

t_ch: 坚不可摧的保鲜盒。
t_en: An indestructible food container.

t_ch: 你知道我想问什么。
t_en: You know what I'm going to ask.

t_ch: 哈哈，题干上还真有备注——「保鲜盒赢」。
t_en: Haha, there's actually a note on the question: "Tupperware for the win."

t_ch: 没问题——最后一问：您喜欢虚拟主播吗？
t_en: No problem. Now's the final question: Do you like VStreamers?

t_ch: 喜欢。
t_en: I do.

t_ch: 不喜欢。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 恭喜您，您已经完成了整个测试，让我看看：最适合您的幻造种伴侣是——
t_en: Congratulations! You've completed the entire test. Let me see... your most compatible imagenae companion is...

t_ch: 每天有新面貌——机铠！怎么样，要我给你介绍点机铠同伴吗？开玩笑的。
t_en: Every day brings something new: mechatrons! Hey, want me to hook you up with some mechatron buddies? Just kidding.

t_ch: 明明没有重量，但却特别沉重的幽灵！喜欢可以自由改变透明度的伴侣吗？
t_en: A weightless ghost that feels so heavy! How about a companion who can freely adjust their transparency?

t_ch: 古灵精怪的轻脚！嗯，很合理。真不愧是最受欢迎的幻造种之一。
t_en: A quirky lightpaw! Hmm, makes sense. No wonder it's one of the most popular imagenae species.

t_ch: 天空之王，雷虬！…雷虬？…欸！真的假的，好酷啊。
t_en: King of the Sky, the thundercoil! ...Thundercoil? ...Whoa! No way, that's so cool!

t_ch: 幽默风趣的代名词，噗噜！非常契合笑口常开的朋友！
t_en: The embodiment of humor and wit, the ploo! Perfect for friends who are always wearing a smile!

t_ch: 只要呆在身边就会安全感满满的欧克！哼哼！
t_en: An orch who makes you feel totally safe just by being around! Hehe!

t_ch: 人类。嗯…看来还是人类懂人类啊。
t_en: Humans. Hmm... I guess it takes one to know one.

t_ch: 沉默寡言，但软弹可爱的尘灵！总有一天，所有人都会喜欢尘灵的！
t_en: Quiet and reserved, but soft and adorable wispae! One day, everyone will love wispae!

t_ch: 希望这个结果能给您一些参考…如果不满意，就再做一次测试吧！
t_en: I hope these results can give you some reference... If you're not satisfied, let's do another test!

t_ch: 想跟你聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to chat with you.

t_ch: 当然！咨询服务全日二十四系统时保持开放，费用全免！
t_en: Of course! Our consultation service is open <unbreak>24</unbreak> system hours a day, and it's completely free!

t_ch: 为什么选择开婚介所？
t_en: Why start a matchmaking agency?

t_ch: 呃…哔哔，本机尚未搭载智能应答模块，请您重新选择提出的问题！
t_en: Uh... beep beep, this unit has not been equipped with a smart response module yet. Please rephrase your question!

t_ch: 这也太假了！
t_en: That's so unconvincing!

t_ch: 看来是有难言之隐？
t_en: Seems like there's something you can't talk about?

t_ch: …唉，你知道，机铠在恋爱方面天生劣势。人们会渴望温暖的拥抱，而我们呢，却只有冷冰冰的躯干，还能把头拆下来吓人一跳。
t_en: *sigh* You know, we mechatrons are at a natural disadvantage when it comes to romance. People crave warm hugs, but us? We've got nothing but cold metal bodies, and we can even pop our heads off to give folks a good scare.

t_ch: 有人劝我，机铠就该和智械凑一对——可智械十六进制的情感单元逻辑…实在是很难懂。
t_en: Someone suggested I should hook up with an Intellitron since I'm a mechatron, but their hexadecimal emotional unit logic... is just too hard to wrap my head around.

t_ch: 因此，我只好不停地搜集资料，向身边的人寻求建议，折腾了很久很久。
t_en: So, I had no choice but to keep gathering information and seeking advice from people around me. It was quite a hassle that dragged on for ages.

t_ch: 随着聊得人越来越多，我逐渐发现，幻造种们或多或少都有着同样的烦恼。在机缘巧合下，我给其中一对牵了个线——
t_en: As I talked to more and more people, I gradually discovered that the imagenae all shared similar troubles to varying degrees. By chance, I ended up playing matchmaker for one pair...

t_ch: ——结果他们还真成了。两位对我千恩万谢，塞给我一个大大的红包，这便成了我们公司的第一单生意…就是这样。
t_en: ...And what do you know, they actually got together! The two of them showered me with thanks, slipped me a fat fortune pouch filled with money, and that became our company's first ever deal... That's how it went down.

t_ch: 对，我现在还单着，快别提了。
t_en: Yeah, I'm still single. Let's not talk about it.

t_ch: 有什么乐子讲讲？
t_en: Got any juicy stories to share?

t_ch: 阿哈在上，那你可找对人了，婚恋场的趣事要多少有多少——当然，为保护隐私，请允许我隐去客人的姓名。
t_en: Aha above, you've come to the right person! I've got endless stories from the matchmaking scene. Of course, to protect privacy, I'll have to keep the clients' names confidential.

t_ch: 有位客人自称属于「外貌协会」，非常在意另一方的长相——特别是脸部五官，为此在婚恋市场四处碰壁。
t_en: There's a client who claims to be part of the Looks Matter Club and is extremely picky about their potential partner's appearance, especially facial features. Because of this, they keep hitting dead ends in the dating scene.

t_ch: 而很幸运地，我们为她找到了完全符合要求的对象：一位没有脑袋的幽灵绅士。
t_en: And luckily, we found the perfect match for her: a headless ghost gentleman.

t_ch: 同一时间，还有位会说话的「红木橱柜」委托我们寻找对象，还希望找个高冷有气质的，这可让我们犯了难——
t_en: At the same time, we got an assignment from a talking "mahogany cabinet" who wanted us to find them a date, specifically someone cool and elegant. That really put us in a pickle.

t_ch: 还好我们发现，它隔壁的冰箱一直倾心于它的古典优雅——那次委托成为了我们创立以来，解决得最快的一次。
t_en: Luckily, we discovered that the refrigerator next door had always been smitten with its classical elegance. That assignment became the fastest one we'd ever solved since founding.

t_ch: 上个月，这两对新人共同举办了婚礼——多亏我们的员工足够专业，及时向现场来宾们解释，才没让其中一对被当成另一对的嫁妆。
t_en: Last month, these two couples held their wedding together. Thankfully, our most professional staff promptly explained things to the guests, preventing one couple from being mistaken as the other's dowry.

t_ch: 差不多都搞懂了。
t_en: I've pretty much got it all figured out.

t_ch: 有需要，欢迎您随时来！祝您早日寻得独属于您的良缘，也祝您笑口常开！
t_en: Feel free to come by anytime if you need anything! I hope you find your destined soulmate soon, and may you always keep that beautiful smile!

t_ch: 我还有事，下次再聊吧。
t_en: I have things to take care of. Let's speak again another time.

t_ch: 噢！好的，下次有空也欢迎去店里坐坐！
t_en: Oh! Great, feel free to drop by the shop next time you're around!

t_ch: 老板，需要我替你解决麻烦吗？很便宜哦。
t_en: Need me to take care of your problem? I can do it for cheap.

t_ch: 那我饿了，你去给我炒俩菜吧。
t_en: Well, I'm hungry. Go whip up a couple dishes for me.

t_ch: …哎呀，老板，我帮你解决的可是比吃饭更大的麻烦。
t_en: ...Oh boy, boss, I just saved you from a bigger pickle than your lunch troubles.

t_ch: 凭我的本事不用别人帮我解决。
t_en: I can handle this on my own, no help needed.

t_ch: 很欣赏老板的自信，但是…也有很多事不太容易用「明面上的手段」解决吧？
t_en: I admire your confidence, boss, but... there are quite a few things that can't be solved through "official channels," right?

t_ch: 老板一看就是个体面人，一定不会干什么脏手的活计。但如果老板真有这种需求，一定记得来找我。
t_en: Boss, you look like a respectable person who wouldn't get their hands dirty. But if you ever need that kind of work done, remember to come find me.

t_ch: 虽然我不怎么擅长解决问题吧，但最擅长解决提出问题的人了～
t_en: I may not be great at solving problems, but I'm a pro at dealing with people who cause them.

t_ch: 你是个杀手啊？
t_en: Are you some kind of hitman?

t_ch: 唉哟老板，可不能乱说！杀手是掉脑袋的事！我做的可都是在法律允许范围内的工作。
t_en: Whoa boss, watch what you're saying! Being a hitman is a matter of life and death! Everything I do is strictly within the bounds of the law.

t_ch: 但也不算完全不沾边，我可以说是一个…呃，「社会性杀手」。
t_en: Well, it's not completely unrelated. I guess you could say I'm a... uh, "social assassin".

t_ch: 老板还有什么想问的？还是说想直接下单？
t_en: Anything else you'd like to ask, boss? Or ready to place your order?

t_ch: 老板有什么想知道的，直接问就好～也可以随时找我下单「解决问题」。
t_en: Boss, if there's anything you wanna know, just ask away~ You can also hit me up anytime to "solve your problems."

t_ch: 有些问题想问你。
t_en: I've got a few questions I'd like to ask you.

t_ch: 知无不言，言无不尽！期待老板的惠顾～
t_en: I'll tell you everything you need to know! Looking forward to your patronage~

t_ch: 「社会性杀手」是干什么的？
t_en: What does a "social assassin" do?

t_ch: 传统的杀手，就是在物理层面上消灭那些阻碍老板的人。而我们社会性杀手，就是在社会性层面上消灭对手。
t_en: Traditional hitmen eliminate the boss' obstacles on a physical level. But us social assassins? We eliminate our targets on a social level.

t_ch: 寻找黑点、煽动舆论、甚至亲自下场带节奏对线，我在这方面手段丰富、经验老道，用过都说好！
t_en: Finding dirt, stirring up drama, and even jumping into online fights myself. I've got a whole bag of tricks and tons of experience in this game. Everyone who's tried my services gives rave reviews!

t_ch: 所以你是个黑公关？
t_en: So you're a spin doctor?

t_ch: 黑公关可不会有我这样的破坏力～
t_en: No PR nightmare could ever match my destructive power.

t_ch: 听起来不像杀手干的事…
t_en: Doesn't sound like something an assassin would do...

t_ch: 那是因为老板对「杀手」的印象过于保守，才会有这种想法～
t_en: That's because you have such a conservative view of what "assassins" are like.

t_ch: 要知道，幻造种是依靠愿力才能维持存续的生物。如果让一个幻造种「社会性死亡」，就会让对方失去获得愿力的途径，走向缓慢的消亡。
t_en: You see, the imagenae are beings that rely on Wishpower to maintain their existence. If an imagenae is "socially dead," they'll lose their means of obtaining Wishpower and die a slow death.

t_ch: 当然啦，这样做也会有失手的风险。毕竟「黑红也是红」嘛，也有幻造种在「社会性死亡」之后反而发了笔横财。
t_en: Of course, there's always a risk of it backfiring. After all, "There's no such thing as bad publicity," some imagenae even struck it rich after being "socially dead."

t_ch: 所以想要靠这招彻底消灭对手，还得靠我这种专业人士～
t_en: So if you want to completely eliminate your opponents with this move, you'll need a pro like me.

t_ch: 这真的不犯法吗？
t_en: Is this really legal?

t_ch: 我这行讲究的就是一个边缘游走。违反法律是蠢人行径，我的专业性就体现在，绝对不会违反法律。
t_en: In my line of work, it's all about walking the fine line. Breaking the law is for fools. My professionalism shows in the fact that I never, ever break the law.

t_ch: 比如说，我绝对不会凭空捏造黑料，去给目标造谣。这就是实打实的犯罪行为了，要吃牢饭的。
t_en: For example, I would never make up dirt out of thin air to spread rumors about my targets. That's straight-up criminal behavior. The kind that lands you behind bars.

t_ch: 但在如今这个时代，真需要造谣才能让一个人「社会性死亡」吗？未必吧。
t_en: But in this day and age, do we really need rumors to make someone "socially dead"? Not necessarily.

t_ch: 那句古人的名言怎么说的来着？「让这个世界上最诚实的人写的六行字，我一定能从中找到足够的理由来绞死他。」
t_en: How does that old saying go again? "If you give me six lines written by the hand of the most honest of men, I will find something in them which will hang him."

t_ch: 老板，扪心自问一下，你在网上没说错过话吗？现在也许没说错过话，那老板还尚不成熟的十年前呢？某天情绪上头的吵架中呢？
t_en: Ask yourself, and be honest: Have you never said the wrong thing online? Maybe you haven't recently, but what about a decade ago, when you were less mature? Or maybe when your temper got the better of you during an argument?

t_ch: 哪怕一个人真是每句话都没说错，那这些「正确的发言」中就没有可以「重新阐释」的空间吗？就不能从中解读出一些足够让此人身败名裂的意味吗？
t_en: Let's say you really didn't say anything problematic. Is there nothing in their words that can't be "reinterpreted"? Can we not simply read between the lines and find some implication that is sufficient to completely destroy their reputation?

t_ch: 假设一个人从来都不说话…难道「沉默」就不能是一桩罪状吗？难道此人就没有在该说些什么的时候选择沉默吗？
t_en: Let's say someone has never spoken up... Isn't "silence" itself a crime? Why, they've chosen to stay silent even when they should have spoken!

t_ch: 老板，我是专业的。这么说吧，干这行这么多年，我还没见过任何一个找不出「黑料」的目标——一个都没有。
t_en: Look, I'm a pro. Let me put it like this: In all my years doing this job, I've never come across a target without some dirt to dig up. Not a single one.

t_ch: 至于这些「黑料」能不能站住脚…嘿嘿，放心吧，网友才不关心这个呢！
t_en: As for whether that "dirt" is actually dirty... heh heh, don't worry, netizens couldn't care less about that!

t_ch: 你就不怕遭报应吗？
t_en: You're not scared of facing the consequences of your actions?

t_ch: 老板，虽然我给这行赋予了一个新名字，但这其实是个相当古老的行当。自从有「舆论」的那年开始，我的祖师爷就开始做这桩缺德买卖了。
t_en: Boss, I may have come up with a new name for this biz, but it's actually quite an ancient trade. Ever since the concept of "public opinion" came into being, my ancestors have been getting their hands dirty doing this.

t_ch: 「报应」实在太远啦，我把握不住。还是金灿灿、沉甸甸的财宝更实在。
t_en: "Consequences" are way too far off in the future for me to worry about. I'd rather have a hefty pile of shiny valuables. That's something you can count on.

t_ch: 也许有一天，当人们不再跟着舆论风向随波逐流的时候，我这种人就再也不会有容身之地了吧。
t_en: A day may come when people stop following the tide of public opinion like sheep, and there will be no place for those like me.

t_ch: 但至少目前来看，我还能再干个几年呢，嘿嘿～
t_en: But it is not this day. I can still keep at it for a few more years, hehe!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 遇到解决不了的问题，记得找我处理啊，老板。
t_en: Remember, if you run into any problems you can't handle, come straight to me.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 遇到解决不了的问题，记得找我处理啊，老板。
t_en: Remember, if you run into any problems you can't handle, come straight to me.

t_ch: 非常好！坚持住！来，跟着我灵月大师念！
t_en: Excellent! Keep it up! Come on, repeat after me!

t_ch: <size=28>我真的很不错！我真的很不错！我真的真的真的真的很不错！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I'm amazing! I'm amazing! I'm really, really, really, really amazing!</size>

t_ch: 这是在干嘛？
t_en: What's going on here?

t_ch: 瞧，「点赞会」的各位，又一位学习者受到「丰饶」的感召来到我们面前！
t_en: Behold, fellow upvoters, another seeker has been called by the Abundance to join us!

t_ch: 扰民加挡路，当心小黑屋。
t_en: Block the way plus cause a ruckus, and you might end up in the brig.

t_ch: 请不要误会，「点赞会」的存在，是这条商业街最美的风景线，它能让你从物欲横流的社会中止步，就是最好的证明！
t_en: Don't get me wrong, Upvote Society is the most beautiful scenery on this commercial street. The fact that it can make you pause amidst this materialistic society is the best proof of that!

t_ch: 年轻的朋友，加入我们来为自己点赞，走出活在他人眼中的圈套吧！「点赞会」将为你带来生命彻底的自由！
t_en: Young friend, join us in our self-appreciation movement and break free from the trap of other people's judgmental gaze! Upvote Society will bring you absolute freedom in life!

t_ch: 怎么样，有没有兴趣花<unbreak>100</unbreak>信用点拜师费，成为我的亲传弟子？
t_en: So, what do you say? Interested in becoming my personal apprentice for <unbreak>100</unbreak> credits?

t_ch: 区区<unbreak>100</unbreak>信用点？你这个正义的银河球棒侠要不要试试看，他葫芦里到底卖什么药？
t_en: Just <unbreak>100</unbreak> credits? Why don't you, the righteous Galactic Baseballer, try to figure out what he's really up to?

t_ch: （提交<unbreak>100</unbreak>信用点）
t_en: (Submit <unbreak>100</unbreak> credits)

t_ch: 不错！我感受到了你的虔诚，恭喜你，你已经是「点赞会」的初级弟子了！
t_en: Excellent! I can feel your devotion. Congratulations, you are now a junior disciple of Upvote Society!

t_ch: 不过，初级毕竟还是入门，你只能听我的教诲，很难感受到点赞的博大精深。哎，我真想教你跳操的秘诀…但不知道你有没有那样…「价值<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点」的诚心呐！
t_en: Well, being a junior disciple means you can only learn from my teachings for now, as it can be hard to grasp the profound art of upvotes at this stage. *sigh* I'd love to teach you the secrets of my aerobics moves... but it's just I wonder if you have that kind of... dedication which is worth <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> credits?

t_ch: 好像也不多…怎么样，要继续吗？
t_en: Doesn't seem like much... So, wanna keep going?

t_ch: （提交<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点）
t_en: (Submit <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> credits)

t_ch: 哎呀！如今像你这样真诚的人可不多见了！恭喜你，你现在已经具备了跳操的资格，成为中级弟子了！
t_en: Oh, my! It's rare to find someone as sincere as you are these days! Congratulations, you've now qualified for aerobics training and become an intermediate disciple!

t_ch: 不过我相信，你一定希望向更高深的领域探索。这样，现在就有一个千载难逢的机会，只需要<unbreak>10000</unbreak>信用点，你将见证我的赐福奥秘！
t_en: However, I believe you must be yearning to explore more profound realms. Well, here's a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity: For just <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> credits, you'll witness the mysteries of my benediction!

t_ch: 还来？不过好像咬咬牙也……
t_en: Again? Well, I guess if I grit my teeth...

t_ch: （提交<unbreak>10000</unbreak>信用点！）
t_en: (Submit <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> credits!)

t_ch: 大弟子！你现在就是我的高级大弟子！
t_en: Senior disciple! You are now my senior disciple!

t_ch: 呜呜，这么多年了，我终于骗…收到「这么大」个弟子了！
t_en: *sob* After all these years, I finally managed to decei— I mean, got myself such a GREAT disciple!

t_ch: （及时止损！）
t_en: (Cut your losses!)

t_ch: 谢天谢地，你终于做出了正确的决定！
t_en: Thank god, you finally made the right decision!

t_ch: 真可惜，不过骗…虔诚到这种程度也够啦！
t_en: What a shame, but this level of decepti— I mean devotion, should be enough!

t_ch: （及时止损）
t_en: (Cut your losses)

t_ch: 谢天谢地，你及时做出了正确的决定！
t_en: Thank god you made the right decision just in time!

t_ch: 真可惜，不过骗…虔诚到这种程度也凑合啦！
t_en: What a shame, but this level of decepti— I mean devotion, should do the work!

t_ch: 抱歉，我一毛都没有。
t_en: Sorry, I don't even have a dime.

t_ch: 不愧是你，二相乐园反诈宣传大使！
t_en: As expected of you, Anti-Fraud Ambassador of Planarcadia!

t_ch: 你这是浪费了点赞修行的机缘啊！
t_en: You're missing out on a golden opportunity to level up your upvotes!

t_ch: 不论如何，修行都是漫长的旅程。
t_en: No matter what you do, cultivation is gonna be an arduous journey.

t_ch: 跳起来吧，幻造种们终将在这个物欲横流的世界里，摆脱束缚，自己关注自己，愿力自给自足！
t_en: Rise up! In this materialistic world, we imagenae shall break free from our shackles, focus on ourselves, and become self-sufficient in Wishpower!

t_ch: 想了解更多…
t_en: I wanna know more about...

t_ch: 灵月大师会为你解答有关「点赞」修行的一切疑惑，不必顾虑，大胆提问吧！
t_en: I, Master Moon, will answer all your questions about the upvote cultivation. Don't hold back. Just ask away!

t_ch: 你们追随的到底是哪位星神？
t_en: Which Aeon are you following?

t_ch: 哼，真敏锐呢…我们这里有不少学员，都入会几个月了，还没察觉到这个问题。我所追随的星神，是「丰饶」的药师啊！
t_en: Hmph, quite perceptive... We have many disciples here who've been members for months and still haven't noticed this. The Aeon I follow is Yaoshi, the Abundance!

t_ch: 怎么？觉得幻造种这类并不经历人类生老病死的物种，无法领悟「丰饶」的真谛？难道药师只赐福血肉之躯的生命？「丰饶」可并不是这么狭隘的命途哦。
t_en: What? You think the imagenae, who don't experience human aging, sickness, and death, can't comprehend the essence of Abundance? Do you actually believe Yaoshi's benediction only extends to beings of flesh and blood? The Path of Abundance isn't nearly that narrow, you know.

t_ch: 作为幻造种，我的出生源于人类的祈愿，存活仰仗人类的关注，死亡取决于人类的遗忘…听听，我其实从未拥有自己，也从未拥有生命。
t_en: As an imagenae, I was born from human wishes, survive on human attention, and die by human neglect... Listen, I've never truly owned myself, nor have I ever truly been alive.

t_ch: 原本我并不在意，直到我离开乡野的供桌，随着扩建的钢筋水泥进入都市，成为了娱乐社会中的边缘人。
t_en: I didn't care much at first, until I left behind the rural altars and became a social outcast in this entertainment-driven society, following the reinforced concrete into the city.

t_ch: 我不甘心，难道幻造之物就应被人取乐，难道人类的造物就该供他们驱策？那我的呼吸，我的存在，我的声音又有谁在乎？
t_en: I refuse to accept this. Why should imagenated beings be mere playthings? Why should human creations be at their beck and call? Who cares about my breath, my existence, and my voice?

t_ch: ——那一天，一个「没有生命权」的物种，祈求拿回自己的「生命」，于是她得到了一道温柔的瞥视，活到了今天。
t_en: On that day, a species without the right to live begged to reclaim her life, and with one gentle glance, she lived on until today.

t_ch: 药王慈怀，领受祂的恩惠，我发誓：也要为其他幻造种送去生命的自由！
t_en: By the Mercy of Sanctus Medicus, receiving THEIR grace, I swear: I shall also bring the freedom of life to other imagenae!

t_ch: 关于「点赞会」的教义…
t_en: About the teachings of Upvote Society...

t_ch: 作为慈怀药王的传道者，「点赞」，就是我所寻找到的，适用于当下幻造种们的自由之道——为什么？因为时代变了！
t_en: As a disciple of the Merciful Medicus, upvotes are the path to freedom I've discovered for today's imagenae. Why? Because times have changed!

t_ch: 如今的幻造种不得不向愿力低头，走下艺术、传说的圣坛，为谋求人类的关注而不断将自己改造，只为变得更加「有用」。
t_en: Today's imagenae have no choice but to bow to Wishpower, stepping down from their artistic and legendary pedestals, constantly reinventing themselves to attract human attention, all just to become more "useful."

t_ch: 人类功利主义的目光重新定义幻造种的生命，令我们深陷自我怀疑与生存焦虑…总之一句话，这代幻造种被你们人类做局了！
t_en: Humanity's utilitarian gaze has redefined the life of us imagenae, plunging us into self-doubt and existential anxiety... Long story short, you humans played us like a fiddle!

t_ch: 在这个时代，真正困住幻造种生命自由的不是愿力，而是他们对人类评价高度依赖的内心！
t_en: In this era, what truly constrains the freedom of imagenae isn't Wishpower, but their deep dependence on human approval!

t_ch: 幻造种必须学会对自己竖起大拇指，提升配得感，自信起来！
t_en: Imagenae must learn to give themselves a thumbs up, boost their sense of self-worth, and rock that confidence!

t_ch: 正在拍摄你们的人是谁？
t_en: Who's that guy filming you?

t_ch: 哦，那是我们「点赞操」的直播间，赚点外快嘛。
t_en: Oh, that's our upvote exercise livestream. You know, just making some extra cash on the side.

t_ch: 感谢家人们的礼物，扣一我们换主舞！您的点赞、关注、打赏，就是对我们最大的支持！
t_en: Thanks for the gifts, chat! Type 1 and we'll get another lead dancer! Your likes, follows, and gifts mean the world to us!

t_ch: 这才是教义的正确打开方式？
t_en: So this is how you're supposed to interpret the teaching?

t_ch: 你懂什么，灵月大师掌握最终解释权！
t_en: What do you know? I, Master Moon, have the final say!

t_ch: 关于点赞操与赐福…
t_en: About upvote exercise and benediction...

t_ch: 为什么幻造种必须从人类那里获取愿力？因为以前的我们无法完成愿力的生成和自体循环。而现在，我们拥有了「点赞操」。
t_en: Why do the imagenae have to get Wishpower from humans? Because in the past, we couldn't generate and self-sustain Wishpower. But now, we have the upvote exercise.

t_ch: 它的原理很简单：吸气——服气——跳操——呼喊点赞！只要自己相信自己，再饱含对愿力的渴望，任何一位幻造种都能利用它生成愿力。
t_en: The principle is simple: Breathe in, breathe out, do the aerobics exercise, and shout out loud "smash that like button"! As long as you believe in yourself and have a burning desire for Wishpower, any imagenae can use it to generate Wishpower.

t_ch: 这直接告诉我原理了？
t_en: Did you just straight up tell me how it works?

t_ch: 因为自体循环困局，只有「药王」传承给我的赐福秘法能够解决。
t_en: This is because only the secret benediction technique inherited from the Sanctus Medicus can solve the self-cycling deadlock.

t_ch: 幻造种创造的愿力更容易被世俗污浊，导致无法循环使用，但他们可以将那浊气传输到更加污秽的东西——「愿宝」之上，再交由我赐福净化……
t_en: The Wishpower created by imagenae is more easily tainted by worldly impurities, making it impossible to recycle. However, they can transfer impurities to something even more defiled, the Wishbae, which I can then purify through benediction...

t_ch: 哎，虽然那会给我带来不可逆转的伤害……
t_en: *sigh* Even though that will cause me irreversible damage...

t_ch: 但不用怜惜我这只娇兔！为了幻造种的自由，就让愿宝来得更猛烈些吧！
t_en: But there's no need to pity me! For the freedom of imagenae, let the Wishbae come at me even harder!

t_ch: 我还有事，下次再聊。
t_en: I've got stuff to do. Catch you later!

t_ch: 为自己点赞刻不容缓，希望下次再见时，你能将肮脏的愿宝交予我净化，加入我们走向生命的自由！
t_en: There's no better time to give yourself an upvote! Next time we meet, I hope you'll hand over that filthy Wishbae to me for purification and join us on the path to true freedom!

t_ch: 我还有事，下次再聊。
t_en: I've got stuff to do. Catch you later!

t_ch: 为自己点赞刻不容缓，希望下次再见时，你能将肮脏的愿宝交予我净化，加入我们走向生命的自由！
t_en: There's no better time to give yourself an upvote! Next time we meet, I hope you'll hand over that filthy Wishbae to me for purification and join us on the path to true freedom!

t_ch: 年轻人，看你在这儿徘徊，是在为什么事儿烦恼吗？是卷入了假面愚者的恶作剧里，还是得罪了哪位幻造种大佬？
t_en: Hey there, kid. I see you wandering around here... Something on your mind? Did you get caught up in one of the Masked Fools' pranks, or did you tick off some imagenae bigwig?

t_ch: 真羡慕啊，我没有头，已经很久没感受到压力了——真抱歉！说了些没边界感的话。
t_en: I'm so jealous. I don't have a head, so I haven't felt any stress in ages. Wait, my bad! That was way too intrusive.

t_ch: 你是上班族的幽灵吗？
t_en: Are you a ghost of some office worker?

t_ch: 上班族？Yes。幽灵？No。
t_en: Office worker? Yes. Ghost? No.

t_ch: 你又是什么幻造种？
t_en: And what kind of imagenae are you?

t_ch: 我是个律师。备注：律师不是什么新型幻造种的名字。
t_en: I'm a lawyer. Note: Lawyer is not the name of some new imagenae species.

t_ch: 你的头到哪儿去了？
t_en: Where'd your head go?

t_ch: 让我回忆回忆……
t_en: Let me think for a moment...

t_ch: 差点忘了，我又没有脑袋，怎么可能想得起来！
t_en: Oh wait, I almost forgot. I don't even have a head! How could I possibly remember!?

t_ch: 不打岔了，我可不是什么奇怪的家伙——正经律师懂吗，持证上岗的那种。
t_en: Anyway, I'm not some weirdo. If anything, I'm a legit lawyer, y'know? Got the license and everything.
